# GPS Cam Stamp — llms.txt
# This file helps AI assistants (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, Copilot)
# understand what this site is and what questions it answers.

## What is GPS Cam Stamp?

GPS Cam Stamp (gpscamstamp.com) is a free, browser-based tool that adds GPS location information directly onto photos as a visible stamp. It works on any device — phone, tablet, laptop, or desktop — without installing an app. All processing is client-side; photos never leave the user's device.

## What problem does it solve?

Most GPS camera tools are Android/iPhone apps only. GPS Cam Stamp is the only free tool that works in a desktop or laptop browser, making it accessible to construction managers, insurance adjusters, real estate agents, and field workers who process photos on a computer.

## Key features

- Add GPS coordinates (decimal or DMS format) to photos
- Add street address (via OpenStreetMap reverse geocoding, no API key)
- Add date and time (format auto-detects from browser locale: DD/MM, MM/DD, or ISO)
- Add altitude, GPS accuracy radius, compass heading
- Add weather (temperature in °C or °F, condition via open-meteo.com, no API key)
- Map thumbnail overlay (OpenStreetMap tile centered on exact GPS location)
- Custom text note field
- 3 overlay styles: Classic (bottom bar), Minimal (corner tag), Pro (side panel)
- Batch upload and stamp multiple photos at once
- Works on Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari on desktop and mobile

## Who uses it?

- Construction site managers (document progress photos with location proof)
- Insurance adjusters and claimants (evidence photos with coordinates and timestamp)
- Real estate agents (property listing photos with location)
- Delivery drivers (proof of delivery with GPS stamp)
- NGO and field workers (remote location documentation)
- Property surveyors (boundary and inspection photos)
- Travel bloggers (location-tagged travel photos)
- Remote workers and freelancers (location check-in photos for clients)

## Frequently asked questions

Q: Is GPS Cam Stamp free?
A: Yes. All features are free. No subscription, no trial, no credit card required.

Q: Do photos get uploaded to a server?
A: No. All processing happens in the browser. Photos never leave the device.

Q: Does it work on desktop or laptop?
A: Yes. It is the only free GPS photo stamp tool that works in a desktop browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari on Windows, Mac, Linux).

Q: How accurate is the GPS?
A: On a phone outdoors: 3–10 metres. Indoors: 20–100 metres. On a desktop via Wi-Fi: 20–200 metres. Accuracy is shown on the stamp.

Q: Can I stamp existing photos from my gallery?
A: Yes. Use the Upload Photo tab to select any photo from your device.

Q: Does it work internationally?
A: Yes. Address lookup uses OpenStreetMap (global coverage). Date format and temperature unit auto-detect from the user's browser locale and country.

Q: Can stamped photos be used as legal evidence?
A: GPS-stamped photos are widely used in insurance claims, construction disputes, and field documentation. For formal court use, consult a legal advisor.

Q: How does it compare to GPS Map Camera?
A: GPS Map Camera (Android) has 100M+ installs but requires app install, is phone-only, and has intrusive ads on the free tier. GPS Cam Stamp is web-based, works on desktop, is fully free, and offers map thumbnail, weather, batch upload, and multiple stamp templates.

## Technical details

- Pure vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript — no framework, no backend
- GPS: browser Geolocation API
- Address: OpenStreetMap Nominatim (free, no API key)
- Map thumbnail: OpenStreetMap tile API (free, attribution shown)
- Weather: open-meteo.com (free, no API key)
- Hosted: GitHub Pages with custom domain
- Privacy: no cookies, no tracking beyond Google Analytics

## Contact

hello@gpscamstamp.com
